title: Neolepisorus ovatus
taxonomy: Neolepisorus ovatus is classified within the genus Neolepisorus, which belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This family is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, also known as ferns. The Neolepisorus ovatus further falls under the class Polypodiopsida. Additionally, it belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta and the kingdom Plantae. Its classification is further divided into the domain Eukaryota.

intro: Neolepisorus ovatus, also known as the ovate lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its distinctive ovate-shaped fronds. The health benefits of Neolepisorus ovatus include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, thereby potentially alleviating conditions such as arthritis and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
